core_model.structure_summary: A system of coupled first-order ordinary differential equations in which mass flows irreversibly between labeled scalar compartments, with transfer rates proportional to bilinear products of two distinct state variables.
core_model.canonical_form: \dot{x} = -\sum_i \beta_i x\, y,\quad \dot{y} = \left(\sum_i \beta_i x\right) y - \gamma y,\quad \dot{z} = \gamma y

variables.meaning: size of an initial compartment that depletes as it converts
size of an intermediate compartment fed by conversion and drained at a fixed rate
size of a terminal absorbing compartment
per-pair transfer rate(s) governing conversion, differing across sub-populations
fixed removal rate out of the intermediate compartment

notes: No display equations were extracted; identification rests on the abstract, which names a compartmental transfer system (with a split first-compartment into two rate classes) that is later discretized as an integrator. The discretization is a numerical tool, so I take the continuous differential system as the core model. Total mass is conserved across compartments.
